extraction.one_line_summary: PETERSEN Mads won the sprint after Quick-Step controlled the entire stage, positioning him perfectly with full team leadout.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step deployed entire team for sustained pace-setting, monopolizing front despite having dominant sprinter—rare full commitment.
CARCAÑO Brian placed second after sitting on PETERSEN wheel for final 500m, indicating pure speed differential matters more.
Sprint teams chased breakaway allowing EVENEPOEL Remco zero defensive work, revealing GC teams willing to concede stage control.

rider_signals.PETERSEN Mads: positive: Speed advantage so overwhelming that CARCAÑO drafting final 500m still couldn't threaten, secured green jersey with 151-point margin
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: Required no effort to defend red jersey as sprint teams controlled pace, recovered from previous crash injuries
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: Considered contesting sprint in final 2km but decided against it, suggesting selective energy conservation
rider_signals.VAN POPPEL Danny: neutral: Mentioned as potential stage contender pre-race but didn't feature in finale, indicating form below expectations
